scenario: Atlas Retailer runs a Camunda 8.8 fulfillment workflow that processes 20000 orders per day. An ad-hoc sub-process must activate only the subset of inner activities (pick, pack, label) whose element IDs appear in a process variable computed from the order profile.
question: Which configuration drives Zeebe to activate the chosen inner elements when the ad-hoc sub-process is entered?

options.text: The completionCondition boolean expression evaluates which inner element IDs should be activated when the ad-hoc sub-process is reached by the token
The activeElementsCollection expression returns a list of element IDs that match the inner elements which should be activated when the sub-process is entered
The cancelRemainingInstances attribute on the sub-process selects which inner element IDs to activate when the ad-hoc sub-process is entered by the token
The outputElement expression returns a list of element IDs to activate when the ad-hoc sub-process is entered, before any inner flow has completed

a.text: Incorrect. The completionCondition boolean expression is evaluated each time an inner element completes to decide whether the sub-process is done; it does not pick which elements to activate.

b.text: Correct. The activeElementsCollection expression evaluates on entering the ad-hoc sub-process and returns a list of element IDs; Zeebe activates the inner elements whose IDs appear in that list.

c.text: Incorrect. The cancelRemainingInstances attribute governs whether active inner instances are cancelled when the completion condition is met; it does not select inner elements to start.

d.text: Incorrect. The outputElement expression is evaluated on each inner flow completion to append a value to the output collection; it has no role in activating inner elements.

explanation: On entering an ad-hoc sub-process, Zeebe evaluates the activeElementsCollection expression. The result must be a list of strings matching inner element IDs, and those elements are activated.
